entries.polished_md: - Grants access to the raid instance [The Trembling Lagoon](https://eq2.fandom.com/wiki/The_Trembling_Lagoon)

entries.zone_name: Ascent of the Awakened: Audience with the Guardian
entries.polished_md:
## Mechanics

The primary danger posed by this Dragon is **Breath of Fire**, which spawns 2 flame blobs for every player or pet within the Area of Effect! This will instantly overwhelm a raid!

## Positioning & Pull

Place everyone well out of AE range, pull the Dragon and the tank must get him nose into a crook near an alcove. Now it is a long slow burn.

- **MT's healers** must joust in between the **Breath of Flame** attacks to heal, then get OUT of AE range before the **50 seconds** expires.
- As soon as the 2 mobs (for the MT) spawn, **ST** runs in, grabs them, and drags them well out of range and, assisted by all the melee'rs, kills them.
- **DPS** can assist a little, but all the long-range DPS (**mages** and **bowmen**) should be on the Dragon.
- Use all hate reducers to make sure the **MT** does not lose agro or it is all over!
- Talk to him to gain entrance to [Maiden's Gulch](https://eq2.fandom.com/wiki/Maiden's_Gulch)

- Scrutinizing a random weapons rack will deliver [Tolapmuj](https://eq2.fandom.com/wiki/Tolapmuj), who you must speak with to attack Pain and [Suffering](https://eq2.fandom.com/wiki/Suffering).
- **Frontal AE** with 100% power drain and mental damage.

## Access

To get to him, use the [Sebilisian Staff of Sounding](https://eq2.fandom.com/wiki/Sebilisian_Staff_of_Sounding) that is dropped by the previous encounter to break the barrier at entrance to the room.

## Pull

> **Pet pull does not work** because the mob cannot be attacked until someone enters room (he will auto-aggro as soon as you enter).

- Use a dedicated puller to pull him out of room to the tank spot (many raids use the corner where you probably killed the group before).
- Try to keep as many people outside the AOE range on pull as possible and debuff him fast.
- Be sure to use all debuffs that debuff the **melee damage** of him; he can hit really hard and **nearly all damage is melee**.

## Mechanics

### AOE / Jousting

The first AOE is a beast, that has to be **jousted**. Timing it is a bit hard because varies slightly.

> Bring a lot of **trauma cure potions** because the healers won't have time to cure trauma from everyone that gets hit (and with people jousting, they might be out of range too).

### 35% Pillar Phase

When he gets to **35%**, some **pillars** will pop in the room where his original position was. You will get zone-wide messages for that.

> While these are up he will **reflect all damage**, so just debuff and heal at that time.

- The player(s) that have the [Sebilisian Staff of Sounding](https://eq2.fandom.com/wiki/Sebilisian_Staff_of_Sounding) (should be some **DPS classes**, no healer or other class that is needed to keep tank up during that time) should move into the room.
- It helps to have **multiples** of these staffs (they are not no-zone) because it increases your chances of destroying the pillar fast.
- Keep collecting them for your next tries if you cannot kill him first time.
- Start using the staffs on the pillars until the pillars are destroyed, which can take some time. You will get a zone-wide message for that again.
- When the pillars are destroyed, finish off named and you are done with the zone.

## Overview

The mob will most likely require more than one visit to conquer. The initial visits will be used for farming. Should you fail at killing the mob you will have to farm once again.

## Required Tasks

1. **Prevent AoEs**
2. **CURE poison** (Dedicated Noxious Remedy or better) — everyone should bring a lot (**100 per**) or stay home.
3. Complete your objective in **15 minutes** or die from AoE.
4. If attempting kill, then **no deaths!!**

## Trash Mobs

The trash mobs drop **Fishman Skin** (fishman suit). They should be given to the parties that can do HUGE spike AoE damage for farming runs. The key here is **repetitive spike damage**. The suits are used to farm and later kill Leviathan.

> Make sure to kill the **last trash mob by the entrance** or feel the wrath of the tentacles.

Trash mobs repop every **2 days**, therefore you can return to continue your adventure.

## Farming

When setting up to farm, your main objective is to acquire as many **Volatile fluids** as possible.

> There are two different fluids — those from Halls of Fate **DO NOT WORK** here.

To acquire fluids, members must be "eaten" by Leviathan. The **Fishman Skin** off of the trash must be worn by a party member to enter and kill mobs within his belly. The fluids are looted from the mobs. It is recommended the bare bones minimum fluid count to attempt a kill is **50**.

Once you have been in the encounter for **15 minutes** OR once your suits are gone, you die from AoE.

## Preventing AoEs

If you do not continue to suit and send people into the stomach, the mob will begin to AoE and you will wipe. Therefore you must **keep someone "suited up" in the water waiting to be eaten at all times**. Once the suits are gone, take the wipe and come back to fight another day.

> **NOTE!!** AoEs are extra bad because an AoE can ruin armor at a rate of **10–20% per AoE**.

## Killing the Leviathan

**Fishman Skin** suits and fluids are required. Suits for this attempt are given to anyone that has huge spell crit as this boosts the damage done to the mob. Also give suits to **2 high melee dps**. Kill the trash and gather the suits; once again last trash should be killed by the entrance or the rear corridor to prevent tentacle damage. You can get to the rear by staying out of the water and running around the right hand side. Note the **globe North of the mob**.

Suit your team up and send them in like before but alternate melee dps with mages ready to fire off fluid vials as quickly as possible. Suited melees keep the center area clear; **mages/SK's burn fluids** as quickly as possible.

### Maximizing Fluid Damage

To maximize damage inflicted by fluids, make sure **single target mitigation debuffs** are kept up on Leviathan continually.

- Undebuffed fluids will do as little as **150k crit damage**
- A fully debuffed dispatched Levi will take **340–520k from fluids**

> Levi does **not** reflect combat arts — only spells — which includes snares and taunts.

- **Single target debuffs** (Murderous Rake, Dispatch, Obliterated Psyche, Condemn, etc) should be kept up continuously for this purpose
- **Avoid "green" encounter debuffs** (Claras Cacophany, Zander's Choral Rebuff, Despoiling Mist) as these reflect back on whole group, not just caster
- If raid is all on same side, **Brigand** can swim through Levi center to get behind for rear debuffs, then swim back to raid without taking much water damage

### Dispelling Reflective Aura

- **Druids** should be casting [Serene Symbol](https://eq2.fandom.com/wiki/Serene_Symbol)
- **Brigands** should be using [Cuss](https://eq2.fandom.com/wiki/Cuss) as frequently as possible

### The Orb at 20%

Apply **"The Greenmist Orb"** to the globe at **20%**. The orb will disappear from inventory once used.

Continue with suits and fluids until mob is dead.

## Additional info on "The Greenmist Orb"

> This zone requires **"The Greenmist Orb"** (the **purple** one) — not to be confused with the **"Green Mist Orb"** (the **green** one) for the epic weapon update.

The person with the orb must be at the globe located on the North side **BEFORE the mob hits 20%**. The globe will become activated and is clickable at the same time as the green message appears on the screen. **Click and activate the globe quickly** or the opportunity is lost.

If you clicked on it successfully you will get another green message at a later time indicating that the orb has been absorbed. If you failed, the raid party will quickly know because the mob will AoE at **1%** and kill all.

> The orb does **not** need to be "dropped" into the globe, nor does the item get clicked within the inventory or on the hotbar.

## Pull

> **Clear centaurs** around this mob or they will come help her.

## Adds

Comes with three [a Hedon bloomseeker](https://eq2.fandom.com/wiki/a_Hedon_bloomseeker) which have a total of **2,100,000 HP**.

## Mechanics

- She has a very mean **focus spell** that does a lot of damage
- She has **AOE** and **short stifles** as well

## Strategy

Buff up and put some wards, **burn down her adds**, and then just ward/heal and **burn Sariah down**.
- This is the non-attackable NPC in Maj'Dul that spawns the dragon form of Barakah. Use a **Gold Dragon scale** to trigger the attackable dragon.

entries.zone_name: The Firemyst Gully: A Gallant Confrontation

entries.polished_md:
## Overview

To defeat the Godking you must also defeat the two mobs that are linked to assist him, [Power of Anuk](https://eq2.fandom.com/wiki/Power_of_Anuk) and [Armor of Anuk](https://eq2.fandom.com/wiki/Armor_of_Anuk).

Three tanks are optimum for this fight, but it can be accomplished with **two tanks**: one high-aggro fighter such as a [Berserker](https://eq2.fandom.com/wiki/Category:Berserker) focusing on the [Power of Anuk](https://eq2.fandom.com/wiki/Power_of_Anuk) and [Armor of Anuk](https://eq2.fandom.com/wiki/Armor_of_Anuk), and a main tank focusing on the Godking.

## Pull & Kill Order

Pull the [Power of Anuk](https://eq2.fandom.com/wiki/Power_of_Anuk) via a **ranged weapon first**, which will bring the Godking and [Armor of Anuk](https://eq2.fandom.com/wiki/Armor_of_Anuk).

> Be sure the tank fighting the Godking **moves him to a corner of the room** away from the other fight.

1. Kill **[Power of Anuk](https://eq2.fandom.com/wiki/Power_of_Anuk)** first — this will remove the ability of the Godking to cast **[Flame Nova](https://eq2.fandom.com/wiki/Flame_Nova)**
2. Kill **[Armor of Anuk](https://eq2.fandom.com/wiki/Armor_of_Anuk)** second — this will drastically reduce the Godking's defense
3. Finish off the **Godking** third

Comes grouped with the following as an adventure party that your raid must beat:
- [Jracol Binari](https://eq2.fandom.com/wiki/Jracol_Binari) - Healer
- [Meldrath Kloktik](https://eq2.fandom.com/wiki/Meldrath_Kloktik) - Mage
- 2 [Froglok Mercenaries](https://eq2.fandom.com/wiki/a_froglok_mercenary) - Fighters
- [Ludmila Kystov](https://eq2.fandom.com/wiki/Ludmila_Kystov) - Scout

Comes grouped with the following as an adventure party that your raid must beat:

- [Jracol Binari](https://eq2.fandom.com/wiki/Jracol_Binari) — **Healer**
- [Blorgok the Brutal](https://eq2.fandom.com/wiki/Blorgok_the_Brutal) — **Fighter**
- 2 [Froglok Mercenaries](https://eq2.fandom.com/wiki/a_froglok_mercenary) — **Fighters**
- [Ludmila Kystov](https://eq2.fandom.com/wiki/Ludmila_Kystov) — **Scout**

- Control-immune.
- Has a knockback — good spot to fight her is behind her throne, right at the wall.
- Has a charm.

entries.zone_name: The Temple of Scale: Pedestal of the Priestess
entries.polished_md:
## Clearing

- Clear all **drakes** on the lower platform in a clockwise or counterclockwise order, leaving the **droags** for a second clearing, as these have a shorter respawn time.
- **KILL FAST**, aiming for clearing in **10-15 minutes**!

> This is a DPS check — raid average should be **13k DPS** or better.

- Move raid to top of stairs and body pull the drakes, again in a clockwise or counterclockwise manner. All can be pulled without aggro'ing Harla Dar.

## Pull

Once all drakes and droags are clear, pull **Harla Dar** to the blocked wall behind the area where she stands (the zone out) and be careful of **knockback**.

- If you have finished the quest, [Taking on Trakanon](https://eq2.fandom.com/wiki/Taking_on_Trakanon), he will offer to teleport you to the entrance of [Trakanon's Lair](https://eq2.fandom.com/wiki/Trakanon's_Lair).

## Pull

- The **MT** will start by clicking on him — if anyone else does, they will be attacked.

## Mechanics

During the fight he will fall asleep **twice** — there are two different dreams that you must deal with. They can happen in any order, and happen at **80%** and **40%**.

> Anyone underneath Xygoz when he falls asleep will be **instantly killed**.

### First Dream — Mini Dragons

- Spawns miniature versions of all the major dragons in the zone.
- They are all **heroic except for Trakanon**, and not very hard.

### Second Dream — Dolls

- Creates a miniature **doll** of everyone in the raid.
- They spawn at the bottom of the ramp to the **North** (entrance) of the room.
- Everyone must pick up the doll of themselves (**double click**).
- The **last person** to do so will be the one that Xygoz attacks when he wakes up.
- The **MT** does not necessarily have to pick up their doll — they can simply aggro lock Xygoz when he awakens. Leaving the MT's doll up for the remainder of the fight has no negative consequences for the raid.
- Once you have double-clicked your doll, jousting back to the rock will keep the area clear for those having a harder time finding the miniature version of their character.

> Note that this step occasionally **bugs**, and may reset the encounter — just keep trying.

## Other

- Aside from the dreams and an **incurable fear**, there are no special tactics to Xygoz.
- Jousting to the rock will keep the raid from suffering the fear effect.
